candidate_problems.text: Quantify the comparative rates of bacterial resistance-information generation and human therapeutic knowledge generation to test whether the proposed asymmetry predicts resistance emergence across pathogens and drug classes.

Evaluate whether interventions aimed at blocking information transfer—such as anti-plasmid strategies or transmission-network disruption—outperform simply increasing drug discovery throughput in preserving antibiotic effectiveness.
Clarify cases where resistance evolves slowly despite heavy antibiotic exposure, and whether those cases falsify or refine the 'environment teaches bacteria' framing.
